title: Mining Pool Shares
description: Mining pool shares are partial proofs of work found under a pool-defined target that is easier than the network target. They let a pool verify that a miner contributed real hash work, measure each miner's proportional contribution, and split payouts when one submitted share also happens to satisfy the actual network target.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Mining
resources.notes: Primary source for how pools use easier share targets, validate miner work, and map submitted shares to payouts.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad background on proof of work, block construction, miner incentives, and the pooled-mining context around share accounting.
